Agency — Formation.rule: An agency relationship is created when a principal manifests assent that an agent act on the principal’s behalf and subject to the principal’s control, and the agent consents.
Agency — Formation.application: Because the principal authorized the agent to act on her behalf and controlled the work, an agency relationship existed.

Agency — Actual and Apparent Authority.rule: Actual authority arises from the principal’s manifestations to the agent, while apparent authority arises from the principal’s manifestations to third parties causing reasonable reliance.
Agency — Actual and Apparent Authority.application: Because the principal’s conduct led the third party to reasonably believe the agent was authorized, apparent authority existed.

Agency — Ratification.rule: A principal may ratify an unauthorized act by knowingly accepting its benefits or affirming the act with full knowledge of material facts.
Agency — Ratification.application: Because the principal accepted the benefits after learning of the transaction, the act was ratified.

Agency — Agent’s Liability.rule: An agent is personally liable when acting without authority, when the principal is undisclosed or partially disclosed, or when the agent expressly assumes liability.
Agency — Agent’s Liability.application: Because the principal was undisclosed, the agent was personally liable on the contract.

Partnership — Formation.rule: A partnership is formed when two or more persons carry on as co-owners of a business for profit, regardless of intent to form a partnership.
Partnership — Formation.application: Because the parties shared profits and jointly controlled the business, a partnership existed.

Partnership — Partner Authority and Liab.rule: Each partner is an agent of the partnership and may bind it in the ordinary course of business, and partners are jointly and severally liable for partnership obligations.
Partnership — Partner Authority and Liab.application: Because the contract was within the ordinary course, the partnership was bound.

Partnership — Fiduciary Duties.rule: Partners owe duties of loyalty and care to the partnership and to each other, including avoiding self-dealing and usurpation of partnership opportunities.
Partnership — Fiduciary Duties.application: Because the partner diverted a partnership opportunity, the duty of loyalty was breached.

Limited Partnerships (LP and LLP).rule: In a limited partnership, general partners manage and are personally liable, while limited partners have limited liability if they do not participate in control; LLPs limit liability for partnership obligations.
Limited Partnerships (LP and LLP).application: Because the limited partner did not exercise control, limited liability applied.

Corporation — Formation.rule: A corporation is formed by filing articles of incorporation with the state, creating a separate legal entity distinct from its shareholders.
Corporation — Formation.application: Because the articles were properly filed, the corporation was validly formed.

Corporation — Promoter Liability.rule: Promoters are personally liable on pre-incorporation contracts unless the corporation adopts the contract and the parties agree to a novation.
Corporation — Promoter Liability.application: Because no novation occurred, the promoter remained liable.

Corporation — Piercing the Corporate Vei.rule: Courts may pierce the corporate veil when shareholders abuse the corporate form through undercapitalization, commingling, or fraud, causing injustice.
Corporation — Piercing the Corporate Vei.application: Because the corporation was undercapitalized and used to perpetrate fraud, the veil was pierced.

Corporation — Fiduciary Duties of Direct.rule: Directors and officers owe duties of care and loyalty to the corporation and its shareholders, subject to the business judgment rule.
Corporation — Fiduciary Duties of Direct.application: Because the decision was informed and made in good faith, the business judgment rule applied.

Corporation — Duty of Loyalty and Confli.rule: Conflicted transactions are voidable unless approved by disinterested directors or shareholders after full disclosure or shown to be fair to the corporation.
Corporation — Duty of Loyalty and Confli.application: Because the transaction lacked disclosure and fairness, it was voidable.

Shareholders — Voting and Management.rule: Shareholders elect directors and vote on fundamental changes but do not manage daily corporate affairs.
Shareholders — Voting and Management.application: Because management authority rests with the board, the shareholder vote was limited.

Shareholders — Derivative Actions.rule: A shareholder may bring a derivative action on behalf of the corporation after making a demand on the board or showing demand futility.
Shareholders — Derivative Actions.application: Because demand was futile, the shareholder could proceed derivatively.

Distributions and Dividends.rule: Corporations may make distributions only if solvent, meaning they can pay debts as they come due and assets exceed liabilities.
Distributions and Dividends.application: Because the corporation was insolvent, the dividend was improper.

Corporate Dissolution.rule: A corporation may dissolve voluntarily, administratively, or judicially, followed by winding up and distribution of assets.
Corporate Dissolution.application: Because the corporation ceased operations, dissolution and winding up followed.

Limited Liability Companies (LLCs).rule: LLCs combine limited liability of corporations with pass-through taxation and flexible management governed primarily by an operating agreement.
Limited Liability Companies (LLCs).application: Because the operating agreement controlled management, the LLC members were bound by its terms.

LLC — Fiduciary Duties and Management.rule: LLC managers and members owe fiduciary duties as defined by statute and the operating agreement, which may modify but not eliminate the duty of loyalty in many states.
LLC — Fiduciary Duties and Management.application: Because the manager engaged in self-dealing prohibited by the agreement, the duty of loyalty was breached.
